reason: Strategic shift toward AI: CEO Mark Zuckerberg stated 2026 is the year AI "dramatically changes how we work," emphasizing "AI-native tooling" to allow single individuals to do the work of large teams.

category: Cloud / Revenue & Health Sciences / SaaS & Virtual Operations / NetSuite India / Engineering
reason: Oracle began mass layoffs via 6 a.m. termination emails signed 'Oracle Leadership' with no prior notice from HR or managers. TD Cowen estimates 20,000-30,000 affected (~18% of workforce). Hardest-hit: Revenue & Health Sciences and SaaS operations (~30% each), plus NetSuite India Development Centre. Cuts expected to free up $8-10B in cash flow to fund $156B AI data center buildout. Oracle disclosed $2.1B restructuring plan in March 2026 SEC filing. Bloomberg reported March 5 that cuts would target 'roles the company expects it will need less of due to AI.' Oracle declined to comment.

category: Non-client-facing / Global service centers / Back office
reason: CEO Georges Elhedery betting on AI to shrink middle and back office footprint. CFO Pam Kaur cited AI cost efficiencies: 'The real shift is really trying to drive operating leverage...focusing on the benefits we can get through AI, whether it's on better productivity around the revenue line or just the cost benefit.'

CEO Kris Marszalek announced 12% layoff citing enterprise-wide AI integration: 'We are joining the list of companies integrating enterprise-wide AI. Companies that do not make this pivot will immediately fail.' Cuts targeted 'roles that do not adapt in our new world.'
Roles eliminated as digital banking tools and AI reduced staffing in support functions.

New CEO Diran Li announced pivot to AI-first organization, cutting human roles across research, data, and content production. Former CEO Eric Turner confirmed numerous employees laid off as company intensifies focus on AI-driven products for institutional clients.

category: Recruiting, Sales, Operations, and Reality Labs
reason: Funding AI Infrastructure: Part of a broader restructuring to offset multi-billion dollar investments in AI models (Llama) and data center projects.
CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes internal memo: restructuring to focus on AI and enterprise growth. Repositioning for the 'AI era.'
CEO Jack Dorsey: 'Intelligence tools we're creating, paired with smaller and flatter teams, are enabling a new way of working which fundamentally changes what it means to build and run a company.'
CEO Tim Steiner: 'We have largely completed a very significant phase of investment in our robotics and automation capabilities.'
Restructuring plan to 'take full advantage of the possibilities offered by AI.' Up to 2,100 jobs impacted by 2027.
CEO Zubin Appoo: 'AI enables greater productivity in less time and with fewer employees.' Integrating AI into customer software and internal operations.
CEO and cofounder, Danny Jenkins, said company has experienced a spike in growth as it responds to a new age of threats in the AI era. Plans to nearly double its headcount in the next 18 months

Spokesperson: 'We are fundamentally reorganizing our internal operations to become an AI-native agentic organization.' And: 'We've integrated advanced AI agents and automation across our core functions -- Sales, Ops, Design, and Marketing. In many areas, tasks that were previously manual are now handled by intelligent systems.' Layoffs phased over six months as AI agents were tested and deployed. Co-founder Saurabh Jain also departed. Company insisted cuts were 'not a reactive cost-cut' but 'a strategic reallocation of resources.'

category: Software developers, HR (AI oversight), customer-facing roles — redefined as AI-management positions
reason: Previously froze ~7,800 back-office roles due to AI (May 2023). Now tripling U.S. entry-level hiring for 2026, but for fundamentally different roles. New hires manage chatbots, oversee AI outputs, and intervene when AI fails — not traditional back-office work. CHRO Nickle LaMoreaux: 'The entry-level jobs from two to three years ago? AI can do most of them.' CEO Krishna (Oct 2025): 'We are probably going to hire more people out of college over the next 12 months than we have in the past few years.'
New AI-driven productivity programme expected to lead to job cuts. Number not specified.
Shift towards AI; cuts primarily affect support staff. Decision followed review of operations aimed at improving efficiency through AI.
AI-driven restructure in collaboration with India's Infosys.

Workforce reduced by roughly 30% since start of 2026 through multiple layoff rounds. Company deploying AI tools to increase productivity. Winklevoss-led exchange lost over $500M in 2025. As of March 1, company employed about 445 people.
Streamlining all end-to-end work processes using automation and AI.
Efficiency-driven cuts; 'choosing to make these changes at a moment of strength.' Further 1,300 planned.

Spokesperson: 'making organizational changes to further deliver on our AI-forward strategy, which includes hiring AI-proficient talent.'

category: Supply chain, digital operations, tech division
reason: AI-driven forecasting reduced staffing needs; automated parts of supply chain and digital operations.

To offset AI infrastructure spending (up to $135B capex in 2026). Reuters reported executives told senior leaders to begin preparing for cuts. Meta spokesperson: 'This is speculative reporting about theoretical approaches.'
Shifting resources toward technology and data-driven systems. AI across investment analysis and operations reduced need for traditional support roles.
Laid off 119 people in an AI-expansion move, per Folha de S. Paulo.

SEC filing stated cuts made 'to reduce operating expenses and optimize the organizational structure in support of long-term growth and in light of AI-driven efficiency improvements.' Expected annual savings of $70-80M.
Part of restructuring to improve digital business; AI-related digital transformation.
Major layoffs following acquisition; generative AI as means of 'fresh agility and scale.'
Restructuring to align with AI push; part of $865M 'business optimization' program. CEO Julie Sweet: 'Those we cannot reskill will be exited.'
Automated non-client-facing work with AI systems. Offered retraining in AI and data analytics.
AI increasingly replacing manual processes in travel insurance division.
AI-driven productivity and automation. Earnings presentation cited 'workforce reductions, platform simplification' and 'adoption and facilitation of artificial intelligence.'
Eliminate administrative positions as automation and AI rolled out. 'Profound changes by digitalisation and AI will lead to greater efficiency.'
CEO Arvind Krishna: AI agents replaced hundreds of back-office roles. Shifted talent toward AI and quantum computing. Layoffs a 'direct outcome of automation.'
AI-driven tools for pricing, scheduling, shipment tracking. Average headcount down 10.8% YoY. 'Decouple headcount growth from volume growth.'
SVP Beth Galetti: 'This generation of AI represents the most transformative technology we've encountered since the Internet.' CEO Jassy warned AI would reduce workforce.
CEO: 'new realities of AI' and diminished traffic from internet search. Declining revenue made cuts unavoidable. AI reshaped student behavior.
Quadrupling its pipeline of entry-level talent in the US with a plan to hire up to 2,000 early-career talent by the end of the year.
First mass layoff in company history. Staff told their roles had been replaced by AI-driven payroll and back-office systems.

Integrating automation and AI into processes; moving manual customer service tasks to automated systems.

Cloudflare announced plans to hire 1,111 interns in 2026, a massive increase from its former 60-person internship program. The expansion is part of the company's push to build AI infrastructure and develop AI-native talent pipelines.
CEO Micha Kaufman: cuts needed to become 'AI-first company.' AI described as 'coming for your job.' Will exclusively hire individuals proficient in AI.
CEO Marc Benioff on podcast: 'I reduced it from 9,000 heads to about 5,000 because I need less heads.' AI agents handling ~50% of customer interactions.
Shifting focus toward AI-driven networking and security products. CFO: 'need to pivot more resources into the fastest-growth areas.'
Cut 2% of workforce; responded to shifting demand and influence of automation and AI on IT roles.
Redirected capital toward AI chips and advanced manufacturing. Automation and AI-driven efficiencies reduced workforce needs.
CEO: aim to 'simplify hiring using AI and technology and data to reduce manual work.' AI changing job search industry.
CEO Satya Nadella memo: company must 'imagine its mission for a new era.' ~30% of code now written by AI. Majority of cuts were programmers. Shifted resources toward AI products.

CEO George Kurtz memo: 'We're operating in a market and technology inflection point, with AI reshaping every industry, accelerating threats, and evolving customer needs.'
"Network Reconfiguration" utilizing increased technology and automation to streamline operations as the company trims Amazon volumes.

Shifting focus towards a greater use of AI in content moderation.

category: Fraud detection, risk management, customer support (~9%)
reason: CEO Alex Chriss: 'drive more focus and efficiency, deploy automation, and consolidate our technology.' Automation expanded across key functions.
SAP announced €2B restructuring: 'In 2024, SAP will further increase its focus on key strategic growth areas, in particular Business AI.' Most positions covered by voluntary leave and reskilling.

Finalizing the AI Pivot: This was the final wave of the 10k plan. Zuckerberg confirmed the "Year of Efficiency" was designed to shift the "single largest investment" into advancing AI across all products.
CEO Philip Jansen: ~10,000 roles to be replaced by AI; rest from fibre rollout completion. Called generative AI 'a significant leap forward.' BT will be 'a leaner business with a brighter future.'
New CEO Margherita Della Valle announced 11,000 cuts over three years to 'regain competitiveness.' Part of broader efficiency and technology modernization.
